source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The current-voltage characteristic of the output terminals A, B (Fig. 3.3) is the same as that of a battery of emf $\varepsilon_{0}$ and internal resistance $r$. Find $r$.
extraction_notes: image entries are PIL object representations rather than file paths or URLs
force_requires_image set true based on '(Fig. 3.3)' and vision_relevance='essential'

image_dependency: question_text explicitly references 'Fig. 3.3', so the needed current-voltage characteristic is in the image
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the problem is not solvable from text alone
the task depends on reading a plotted characteristic/graph rather than only symbolic text
multi_step: solving for internal resistance r from an I-V characteristic typically requires extracting at least one or more graph features and mapping them to the battery model
the prompt invokes equivalence to a battery with emf and internal resistance, which suggests a model-interpretation step plus a calculation/inference step
answer_type=short_text with no choices means the solver likely performs free-form reasoning rather than simple option matching
verifiability: answer_text is a specific numeric quantity ('$4.8 \\Omega$'), which is easy to compare against a target value
answer_manifest shows answer_present=true and answer_type=short_text, supporting deterministic answer checking
registry_passed=true and image exists, so the core assets needed for later verification are available
because the result comes from graph reading, verification is fairly strong but may still depend on reading precision from the figure

metadata.reasoning_chain: The equivalent internal resistance is the resistance when the battery is shorted, i.e., the parallel combination of the resistances: $r=\frac{6\times 24}{6+24}=4.8 \Omega$.
